title: Dust Attack
description: A dust attack sends trace-value outputs to targets so later spending can link their wallets or contaminate coin-control decisions. It exploits the gap between a policy threshold for tiny outputs and the privacy meaning of spending them.

resources.title: Bitcoin Dev Project: Dust Attack explained
resources.notes: Learner-facing explanation of dusting as a privacy attack.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Transactions
resources.notes: Canonical context for dust thresholds and transaction output policy.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Reference background for UTXOs, transaction construction, and privacy tradeoffs.
